title: Ceiba pentandra
intro: Ceiba pentandra, also known as the Kapok tree, is a large deciduous tree native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The tree has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ceiba pentandra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ceiba pentandra is often used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties.
taxonomy: Ceiba pentandra is classified as a species within the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This species is further categorized as a genus within the order Malvales, which includes other flowering plants. Ceiba pentandra is a member of the kingdom Plantae, indicating its status as a vascular plant. The species is also classified as a dicotyledon, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage. Additionally, Ceiba pentandra is a tree species, classified as a dicot tree within the class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Ceiba pentandra, also known as the Kapok tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The extracts from its bark, leaves, and seeds have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, the tree's sap is used to treat skin conditions and wounds, while its leaves are believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties. Additionally, the seeds of the Ceiba pentandra are said to have diuretic and laxative effects, making them useful in treating digestive issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these medicinal uses.

properties: Ceiba pentandra is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves and bark are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions. constituents: Ceiba pentandra, also known as the Kapok tree, contains a variety of active constituents, including alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids. The tree's seeds are rich in a fatty oil called kapok oil, which has been used for its emollient and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the leaves and bark of the tree are rich in saponins, which may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The tree's roots and stems contain a variety of phenolic compounds, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These active constituents may contribute to the tree's traditional uses in medicine and as a natural remedy.

side_effects: Ceiba pentandra, also known as the Kapok tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, but its use may lead to some adverse effects. The tree's bark, seeds, and leaves are believed to cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, when ingested.
